class  GenericSolverResults
 A type-erased version of the main functionality of SolverResults. Can be accepted by consumers that don't need deep access to the internals (so, the usual ones). As we have now two kinds of solver-results (SolverResults and IdBasedSolverResults), we need a common way of accessing them. More...

Typedefs

template<is_flowfunction_v FF>
using FlowFunctionPtrTypeOf = std::shared_ptr< FF >
 Given a flow-function type FF, returns a (smart) pointer type pointing to FF.
 
template<typename D , typename Container = std::set<D>>
using FlowFunctionPtrType = FlowFunctionPtrTypeOf< FlowFunction< D, Container > >
 
template<typename Problem >
using IDESolver_P = IDESolver< typename Problem::ProblemAnalysisDomain, typename Problem::container_type >
 
template<typename Problem >
using IFDSSolver_P = IFDSSolver< typename Problem::ProblemAnalysisDomain, typename Problem::container_type >
 
using IDESolverConfig = WithComputeValues< IDESolverConfigBase, true >
 
using IFDSSolverConfig = WithComputeValues< IDESolverConfigBase, false >
 
using IDESolverConfigWithStats = WithStats< IDESolverConfig, true >
 
using IFDSSolverConfigWithStats = WithStats< IFDSSolverConfig, true >
 
using IFDSSolverConfigWithStatsAndGC = WithGCMode< IFDSSolverConfigWithStats, JumpFunctionGCMode::Enabled >
 
template<typename Problem , unsigned K>
using InterMonoSolver_P = InterMonoSolver< typename Problem::ProblemAnalysisDomain, K >
 
template<typename Problem >
using IntraMonoSolver_P = IntraMonoSolver< typename Problem::ProblemAnalysisDomain >
 
template<typename N >
using FlowPathSequence = std::vector< FlowPath< N > >
 
using DefaultPathTracingFilter = PathTracingFilter< detail::False2, detail::False2 >
 
template<typename AnalysisDomainTy >
using WithBinaryValueDomain = std::conditional_t< detail::HasBinaryValueDomain< AnalysisDomainTy >::value, AnalysisDomainTy, detail::WithBinaryValueDomainExtender< AnalysisDomainTy > >
 
using LLVMBasedCallGraph = CallGraph< const llvm::Instruction *, const llvm::Function * >
 
template<typename Derived >
using SparseLLVMBasedCFGProvider = SparseCFGProvider< Derived, const llvm::Function *, const llvm::Value * >
 
using IDEInstInteractionAnalysis = IDEInstInteractionAnalysisT<>
 
using LLVMIFDSAnalysisDomainDefault = WithBinaryValueDomain< LLVMAnalysisDomainDefault >
 An AnalysisDomain that specializes sensible defaults for LLVM-based IFDS analysis.
 
using LLVMAliasIteratorRef = AliasIteratorRef< const llvm::Value *, const llvm::Instruction * >
 
using LLVMAliasInfoRef = AliasInfoRef< const llvm::Value *, const llvm::Instruction * >
 
using LLVMAliasInfo = AliasInfo< const llvm::Value *, const llvm::Instruction * >
 
using LLVMPointsToIteratorRef = PointsToIteratorRef< const llvm::Value *, const llvm::Value *, const llvm::Instruction * >
 
using LLVMPointsToIterator = PointsToIterator< const llvm::Value *, const llvm::Value *, const llvm::Instruction * >
 
using SVFBasedPointsToInfo = PointsToInfo< SVFPointsToInfoTraits >
 
using SVFBasedPointsToInfoRef = PointsToInfoRef< SVFPointsToInfoTraits >
 
using SVFBasedPointsToIterator = PointsToIterator< const llvm::Value *, uint32_t, const llvm::Instruction * >
 
using SVFBasedPointsToIteratorRef = PointsToIteratorRef< const llvm::Value *, uint32_t, const llvm::Instruction * >
 
using FunctionRange = llvm::iterator_range< llvm::mapped_iterator< llvm::Module::const_iterator, Ref2PointerConverter< llvm::Function > > >
 
template<typename T >
using ByConstRef = std::conditional_t< CanEfficientlyPassByValue< T >, T, const T & >
 
template<typename T >
using ByMoveRef = std::conditional_t< CanEfficientlyPassByValue< T >, T, T && >
 
template<typename IdT >
using IotaRangeType = llvm::iterator_range< IotaIterator< IdT > >
 
template<typename T >
using Nullable = std::conditional_t< std::is_convertible_v< T, bool >, T, std::optional< T > >
 
template<typename T >
using RepeatRangeType = llvm::iterator_range< RepeatIterator< T > >
 
template<typename Container >
using ElementType = typename detail::ElementType< Container >::type
 

Enumerations

enum class  AnalysisStrategy { None , ANALYSIS_STRATEGY_TYPES }
 
enum class  CallGraphAnalysisType { CALL_GRAPH_ANALYSIS_TYPE , Invalid }
 
enum class  SpecialMemberFunctionType { SPECIAL_MEMBER_FUNCTION_TYPES }
 
enum class  EdgeFunctionAllocationPolicy { SmallObjectOptimized , DefaultHeapAllocated , CustomHeapAllocated }
 
enum class  SolverConfigOptions : uint32_t {
  None = 0 , FollowReturnsPastSeeds = 1 , AutoAddZero = 2 , ComputeValues = 4 ,
  RecordEdges = 8 , EmitESG = 16 , ComputePersistedSummaries = 32 , All = ~0U
}
 
enum class  ESGEdgeKind {
  Normal , Call , CallToRet , SkipUnknownFn ,
  Ret , Summary
}
 
enum class  EdgeFunctionKind {
  Normal , Call , Return , CallToReturn ,
  Summary
}
 
enum class  JumpFunctionGCMode { Disabled , Enabled , EnabledAggressively }
 
enum class  BinaryDomain { BOTTOM = 0 , TOP = 1 }
 
enum class  SecureHeapFact { ZERO , INITIALIZED }
 
enum class  SecureHeapValue { TOP , INITIALIZED , BOT }
 
enum class  CSTDFILEIOState {
  TOP = 42 , UNINIT = 0 , OPENED = 1 , CLOSED = 2 ,
  ERROR = 3 , BOT = 4
}
 
enum class  OpenSSLEVPKDFCTXState {
  TOP = 42 , UNINIT = 5 , CTX_ATTACHED = 1 , PARAM_INIT = 2 ,
  DERIVED = 3 , ERROR = 4 , BOT = 0
}
 
enum class  OpenSSLEVPKDFState {
  TOP = 42 , UNINIT = 0 , KDF_FETCHED = 1 , ERROR = 2 ,
  BOT = 3
}
 
enum class  OpenSSLSecureHeapState {
  TOP = 42 , BOT = 0 , UNINIT = 1 , ALLOCATED = 2 ,
  ZEROED = 3 , FREED = 4 , ERROR = 5
}
 
enum class  SVFPointsToAnalysisType { DDA = int(AliasAnalysisType::SVFDDA) , VFS = int(AliasAnalysisType::SVFVFS) }
 
enum class  TaintCategory { None , Source , Sink , Sanitizer }
 
enum class  DataFlowAnalysisType { None , DATA_FLOW_ANALYSIS_TYPES }
 
enum class  AliasAnalysisType { ALIAS_ANALYSIS_TYPE , Invalid }
 
enum class  AliasResult { ALIAS_RESULT_TYPE }
 
enum class  AnalysisProperties { None = 0 , FlowSensitive = (1 << 0) , ContextSensitive = (1 << 1) , FieldSensitive = (1 << 2) }
 
enum  SeverityLevel { INVALID }
 
enum class  PAMM_SEVERITY_LEVEL { Off = 0 , Core , Full }
 Defines the different level of severity of PAMM's performance evaluation. More...
 
enum class  Soundness { SOUNDNESS_FLAG_TYPE , Invalid }

 Returns a string representation of a LLVM Value.
 
std::string llvmIRToStableString (const llvm::Value *V)
 Similar to llvmIRToString, but removes the metadata from the output as they are not always stable. Prefer this function over llvmIRToString, if you are comparing the string representations of LLVM iR instructions.

 Same as llvmIRToString) but tries to shorten the resulting string.

template<typename G >
SCCDependencyGraph< typename GraphTraits< G >::vertex_t > computeSCCDependencies (const G &Graph, const SCCHolder< typename GraphTraits< G >::vertex_t > &SCCs)
 Creates a graph based on the given input Graph, collapsing all SCCs to single nodes. The resulting graph is always a DAG, i.e., it contains no cycles.
 
template<typename GraphNodeId >
SCCOrder< GraphNodeId > computeSCCOrder (const SCCHolder< GraphNodeId > &SCCs, const SCCDependencyGraph< GraphNodeId > &Callers)
 Computes a topological order of the nodes in the given dependency-graph.

◆ JumpFunctionGCMode

enum class psr::JumpFunctionGCMode
strong
Enumerator
Disabled 

Perform no semantic garbage collection on jump functions.

Enabled 

Perform JF GC, but do not delete any elements from the SolverResults, so all SolverResults are still present, only the edge-values are not computed everywhere

EnabledAggressively 

Perform JF GC as in 'Enabled', but also delete entries from the SolverResults

Definition at line 14 of file StaticIDESolverConfig.h.

◆ buildLLVMBasedCallGraph() [1/4]

LLVMBasedCallGraph psr::buildLLVMBasedCallGraph ( const LLVMProjectIRDB &  IRDB,
Resolver &  CGResolver,
llvm::ArrayRef< const llvm::Function * >  EntryPoints,
Soundness  S = Soundness::Soundy 
)

Constructs a call-graph using the given CGResolver to resolve indirect calls.

Uses a fixpoint iteration, if CGResolver.mutatesHelperAnalysisInformation() returns true and the soundness S is not Soundness::Unsound.

Parameters
IRDBThe IR code where the call-graph should be based on
CGResolverThe resolver to use for resolving indirect calls.
EntryPointsThe functions, where the call-graph construction should start. The resulting call-graph will only contain functions that are (transitively) reachable from the entry-points.
SThe soundness level. May be used to trade soundness for performance.

◆ buildLLVMBasedCallGraph() [2/4]

LLVMBasedCallGraph psr::buildLLVMBasedCallGraph ( const LLVMProjectIRDB &  IRDB,
Resolver &  CGResolver,
llvm::ArrayRef< std::string >  EntryPoints,
Soundness  S = Soundness::Soundy 
)

Constructs a call-graph using the given CGResolver to resolve indirect calls.

Uses a fixpoint iteration, if CGResolver.mutatesHelperAnalysisInformation() returns true and the soundness S is not Soundness::Unsound.

Parameters
IRDBThe IR code where the call-graph should be based on
CGResolverThe resolver to use for resolving indirect calls.
EntryPointsNames of the functions, where the call-graph construction should start. The resulting call-graph will only contain functions that are (transitively) reachable from the entry-points.
SThe soundness level. May be used to trade soundness for performance.

◆ buildLLVMBasedCallGraph() [3/4]

◆ computeSCCDependencies()

template<typename G >
SCCDependencyGraph< typename GraphTraits< G >::vertex_t > psr::computeSCCDependencies ( const G &  Graph,
const SCCHolder< typename GraphTraits< G >::vertex_t > &  SCCs 
)

Creates a graph based on the given input Graph, collapsing all SCCs to single nodes. The resulting graph is always a DAG, i.e., it contains no cycles.

Definition at line 237 of file SCCGeneric.h.

References psr::BitSet< IdT, BitVectorTy >::erase(), psr::BitSet< IdT, BitVectorTy >::foreach(), Ret, and psr::BitSet< IdT, BitVectorTy >::size().

◆ computeSCCOrder()

template<typename GraphNodeId >
SCCOrder< GraphNodeId > psr::computeSCCOrder ( const SCCHolder< GraphNodeId > &  SCCs,
const SCCDependencyGraph< GraphNodeId > &  Callers 
)

Computes a topological order of the nodes in the given dependency-graph.

Uses a simple, recursive postorder-DFS search to find a topological ordering.

References Ret, and psr::SCCHolder< GraphNodeId >::size().

◆ computeSCCs()

template<typename G , bool Iterative = true>
SCCHolder< typename GraphTraits< G >::vertex_t > psr::computeSCCs ( const G &  Graph,
std::bool_constant< Iterative >  = {} 
)

Compute SCCs adapted from the paper "A Space-Efficient Algorithm for Finding Strongly Connected Components", Pearce 2015, DOI: https://doi.org/10.1016/j.ipl.2015.08.010

Template Parameters
GThe graph-type
IterativeWhether to use the iterative or recursive variant of the algorithm (default: true)
Parameters
GraphThe graph for with to compute SCCs and topological ordering

std::bool_constant< Iterative >  = {} 
)

Compute SCCs and a topological ordering on the SCCs, adapted from the paper "A Space-Efficient Algorithm for Finding Strongly Connected Components", Pearce 2015, DOI: https://doi.org/10.1016/j.ipl.2015.08.010

Template Parameters
GThe graph-type
IterativeWhether to use the iterative or recursive variant of the algorithm (default: true)
Parameters
GraphThe graph for with to compute SCCs and topological ordering

Only support DDA for now, as VFS seems to not support getRevPts().

const EdgeFunction< L > &  OtherFunction 
)

Default implementation of EdgeFunction::join. Considers AllBottom, AllTop and EdgeIdentity for OtherFunction as well as This==OtherFunction. Joining with EdgeIdentity will overapproximate to (AllBottom if N==0, else JoinEdgeFunction).

◆ getMetaDataID()

std::string psr::getMetaDataID ( const llvm::Value *  V)

Returns the ID of a given LLVM Value.

Only Instructions and GlobalVariables have 'real' ID's, i.e. annotated meta data. Formal arguments cannot be annotated with metadata in LLVM. Therefore, a formal arguments ID will look like this: <function_name>.<argument>

ZeroValue will have -1 as ID by default.

Returns
Meta data ID as a string or -1, if it's not an Instruction, GlobalVariable or Argument.

Referenced by psr::Z3BasedPathSensitivityManager< AnalysisDomainTy >::pathsTo().

const llvm::Module * psr::getModuleFromVal ( const llvm::Value *  V)

Returns the LLVM Module to which the given LLVM Value belongs to.

Parameters
VLLVM Value.
Returns
LLVM Module or nullptr.

◆ intersectWith() [1/3]

template<typename T >
void psr::intersectWith ( BitVectorSet< T > &  Dest,
const BitVectorSet< T > &  Src 
)

Definition at line 314 of file BitVectorSet.h.

◆ intersectWith() [2/3]

template<typename ContainerTy , typename OtherContainerTy >
requires (!has_erase_iterator_v<ContainerTy>)
void psr::intersectWith ( ContainerTy &  Dest,
const OtherContainerTy &  Src 
)

Computes the set-intersection of the potentially unordered sets Dest and Src and stores the result back in Dest.

This function should work on all types of sets as long as they provide the type value_type specifying, which type their elements have. By-reference iteration is required, but the elements do not have any requirements, although the performance is probably higher for small elements that are trivially copyable.

This whole functionality is only for computing the set-intersection of Dest and Src storing the result in-place in Dest. It would be a big win, if the STL would provide us with in-place set operations and those that do not require the sets to be sorted...

Definition at line 83 of file Utilities.h.

const OtherContainerTy &  Src 
)

◆ mapFactsAlongsideCallSite()

template<typename D = const llvm::Value *, typename Container = std::set<D>, typename Fn = TrueFn, typename DCtor = DefaultConstruct<D>>
requires std::is_invocable_r_v<bool, Fn, const llvm::Value *>
auto psr::mapFactsAlongsideCallSite ( const llvm::CallBase *  CallSite,
Fn &&  PropagateArgs = {},
bool  PropagateGlobals = true,
DCtor &&  FactConstructor = {} 
)

A flow function that serves as default-implementation for the call-to-return flow function. For more details on the use-case of call-to-return flow functions, see the documentation of FlowFunction::getCallToRetFlowFunction().

Propagates all dataflow facts unchanged, except for global variables and arguments of the specified call-site. Global variables are propagated only if PropagateGlobals is set to true; for the argument values the PropagateArgs function defines on a per-arg basis whether the respective argument should be propagated or killed. By default (when not specifying PropagateArgs), all parameters are propagated unchanged.

For analyses that propagate values via reference parameters in the return flow function, it is useful to kill the respective arguments here to enable strong updates.

Definition at line 58 of file LLVMFlowFunctions.h.

◆ mapFactsToCallee()

template<typename D = const llvm::Value *, typename Container = std::set<D>, typename Fn = std::equal_to<D>, typename DCtor = DefaultConstruct<D>>
requires std::is_invocable_r_v<bool, Fn, const llvm::Value *, D>
FlowFunctionPtrType< D, Container > psr::mapFactsToCallee ( const llvm::CallBase *  CallSite,
const llvm::Function *  DestFun,
Fn &&  PropagateArgumentWithSource = {},
DCtor &&  FactConstructor = {},
bool  PropagateGlobals = true,
bool  PropagateZeroToCallee = true 
)

A flow function that serves as default implementation of the call flow function. For more information about call flow functions, see FlowFunctions::getCallFlowFunction().

Propagates the arguments of the specified call-site into the callee function if the function PropagateArgumentWithSource evaluates to true when invoked with the argument and a currently holding dataflow fact. Global variables are propagated into the callee function only, if the flag PropagateGlobals is set to true. The special zero (Λ) value gets propagated into the callee function if PropagateZeroToCallee is true. For most analyses it makes sense to propagate Λ everywhere.

Given a call-site cs: r = fun(..., ax, ...) and a function prototype fun(..., px, ...). Further let f = mapFactsToCallee(cs, fun, ...). Then for any dataflow fact x: f(Λ) = {Λ} if PropagateZeroToCallee else {}, f(ax) = {px} if PropagateArgumentWithSource(ax, ax) else {}, f(g) = {g} if g is GlobalVariable && PropagateGlobals else {}, f(x) = {px} if PropagateArgumentWithSource(ax, x) else {}.

Note
Unlike the old version, this one is only meant for forward-analyses

Definition at line 133 of file LLVMFlowFunctions.h.

◆ mapFactsToCaller()

template<typename D = const llvm::Value *, typename Container = std::set<D>, typename FnParam = std::equal_to<D>, typename FnRet = std::equal_to<D>, typename DCtor = DefaultConstruct<D>, typename PostProcessFn = IgnoreArgs>
requires (std::is_invocable_r_v<bool, FnParam, const llvm::Value *, D> && std::is_invocable_r_v<bool, FnRet, const llvm::Value *, D>)
FlowFunctionPtrType< D, Container > psr::mapFactsToCaller ( const llvm::CallBase *  CallSite,
const llvm::Instruction *  ExitInst,
FnParam &&  PropagateParameter = {},
FnRet &&  PropagateRet = {},
DCtor &&  FactConstructor = {},
bool  PropagateGlobals = true,
bool  PropagateZeroToCaller = true,
PostProcessFn &&  PostProcess = {} 
)

A flow function that serves as default-implementation of the return flow function. For more information about return flow functions, see FlowFunctions::getRetFlowFunction().

Propagates the return value back to the call-site and based on the PropagateParameter predicate propagates back parameters holding as dataflow facts. The resulting out-set of dataflow facts can be post-processed if necessary.

Let a call-site cs: r = fun(..., ax, ...) a function prototype fun(..., px, ...) and an exit statement exit: return rv. Further given a flow function f = mapFactsToCaller(cs, exit, ...). Then for all dataflow facts x holding at exit: f(rv) = {r} if PropagateRet(rv, rv) else {}, f(px) = {ax} if PropagateParameter(px, px) else {}, f(g) = {g} if PropagateGlobals else {}, f(Λ) = {Λ} if PropagateZeroToCaller else {}, f(x) = ({ax} if PropagateParameter(ax, x) else {}) union ({r} if PropagateRet(rv, x) else {}).

Definition at line 240 of file LLVMFlowFunctions.h.

◆ matchesSignature() [1/2]

bool psr::matchesSignature ( const llvm::Function *  F,
const llvm::FunctionType *  FType,
bool  ExactMatch = true 
)

Returns true if the provided function and the function type are both not null and have the same number of parameters and the same return type. If the argument ExactMatch is set to true, which it is by default, the two provided arguments must also have the same type for each argument, for the function to return true.

Note
This function is less useful in practice than you may think. Consider using isConsistentCall() instead.

◆ matchesSignature() [2/2]

bool psr::matchesSignature ( const llvm::FunctionType *  FType1,
const llvm::FunctionType *  FType2 
)

Returns true iff the provided functions are both not null and have the same number of paramters, the same return type and each parameter of both functions has the same type aswell.

Note
This function is less useful in practice than you may think. Consider using isConsistentCall() instead.

◆ remove_by_index() [3/3]

template<typename It , typename EndIt , typename IdxIt , typename IdxEndIt >
It psr::remove_by_index ( It  First,
EndIt  Last,
IdxIt  FirstIndex,
IdxEndIt  LastIndex 
)

Based on the reference implementation of std::remove_if https://en.cppreference.com/w/cpp/algorithm/remove and optimized for the case that a sorted list of indices is given instead of an unary predicate specifying the elements to be removed.

Random-access version inspired from "https://codereview.stackexchange.com/a/207056" and slightly changed

◆ strongUpdateStore() [1/2]

template<typename Container = std::set<const llvm::Value *>>
FlowFunctionPtrType< const llvm::Value *, Container > psr::strongUpdateStore ( const llvm::StoreInst *  Store)

A flow function that models a strong update on a memory location modified by a store instruction. Similar to transferFlow.

Given a flow function f = strongUpdateStore(store a to b), for all holding dataflow facts x: f(b) = {}, f(a) = {a, b}, f(x) = {x}.

In the exploded supergraph it may look as follows: 

          x   a   b  ...
          |   |\  |
          |   | \    ...

store a to b | | \ ... v v v x a b ...

Definition at line 416 of file LLVMFlowFunctions.h.

References Source.

◆ strongUpdateStore() [2/2]

template<typename Fn , typename Container = std::set<const llvm::Value *>>
requires std::is_invocable_r_v<bool, Fn, const llvm::Value *>
FlowFunctionPtrType< const llvm::Value *, Container > psr::strongUpdateStore ( const llvm::StoreInst *  Store,
Fn &&  GeneratePointerOpIf 
)

A flow function that models a strong update on a memory location modified by a store instruction

Given a flow function f = strongUpdateStore(store a to b, pred), for all holding dataflow facts x: f(b) = {}, f(x) = {x, b} if pred(x) else {x}.

Definition at line 339 of file LLVMFlowFunctions.h.

References Source.
